name: Aaron Copland
description: American composer, composition teacher, writer, and conductor (1900-1990)
abstract: Aaron Copland ( KOHP-lənd; November 14, 1900 – December 2, 1990) was an American composer, critic, writer, teacher, pianist, and conductor of his own and other American music. Copland was referred to by his peers and critics as the "Dean of American Music".

about_quotes.speaker: Ted Piltzecker
about_quotes.evidence: I happen to teach melody writing... I was surprised when I first read that Aaron Copland, who wrote some really incredible melodies, and he said...
about_quotes.doc_title: Ted Piltzecker interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 10, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Al Chez
about_quotes.evidence: Alec Baldwin likes classical music. Okay let’s play “Simple Gifts” by Aaron Copland.
about_quotes.doc_title: Al Chez interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, February 13, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Mike Longo
about_quotes.evidence: Look man, Aaron Copland said, “The art of classical music is the art of melody.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Mike Longo interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: John Heard
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee only responds to the interviewer's mention of Aaron Copland with agreement ('Yeah, that’s right') and pivots to discussing Duke Ellington. There is no evidence of a personal relationship, meeting, or collaboration between John Heard and Aaron Copland.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Heard interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 4, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Akiyoshi Toshiko
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] I said Aaron, Aaron Copland. [INTERVIEWEE] Oh, yes, right. That‟s what they said and it was … but he, he wanted to, so…. But his November Steps is the classic one and he liked to use the biwa
about_quotes.doc_title: Akiyoshi Toshiko — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Alan Raph
about_quotes.evidence: I worked with Aaron not much, with a little orchestra society... Aaron came into conduct his theater music and I think that’s the only time I ever worked for him, or with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Alan Raph interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001
